title: Carelia litorale
taxonomy: Carelia litorale is classified as a species within the family Zosterophyllaceae, which is a group of extinct, ancient plants. The classification of Carelia litorale is based on its fossil record and morphological characteristics. This plant is considered a part of the ancient plant group, and its classification is still evolving as new research emerges. Carelia litorale is often classified as a type of early land plant, likely dating back to the Devonian period. Further studies are needed to confirm the exact classification and position of Carelia litorale within the plant kingdom.
uses: Carelia litorale, a species of flowering plant, has been found to possess certain medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine.
